name: Evans, Roxanne
resolved_employer: Retired journalist/communications professional (retired) — Austin American-Statesman (first Black member of the editorial board / Chief Editorial Writer), deputy press secretary to Gov. Ann Richards, PR Director for the Texas Federation of Teachers, Chief Communications Officer for DC Public Schools, communications staff at Austin ISD and City of Austin
evidence: Long-time East Austin resident (zip 78723 matches); prominent former Austin journalist and PR professional — became the first Black editorial-board member/Chief Editorial Writer at the Austin American-Statesman in the 1980s, later deputy press secretary for Texas Governor Ann Richards, PR Director for the Texas Federation of Teachers, and Chief Communications Officer for DC Public Schools. Ran for Austin ISD District 1 trustee in 2022 (campaign finance report on file with AISD) and serves on the board of the Austin History Center Association. No AIPAC/J Street, oil & gas, gun-lobby, military-defense ties, or TX lobbyist registration found; her PR role for the Texas Federation of Teachers (a teachers' labor union) is noted but is not one of the tracked spectrum categories.

name: Hill, Todd
resolved_employer: Urban Institute (Housing Finance Policy Center) — Director, Policy and Programs, Housing and Communities Division
evidence: Occupation 'Director' + employer 'Urban Institute' match his public Urban Institute bio exactly: Todd Hill (Hill-Jones) is Director, Policy and Programs, Housing and Communities Division, leading the Housing Finance Policy Center. He returned to Urban Institute in May 2022 after 3 years at Noodle (ed-tech); before that he spent 6 years as senior director of government affairs at the Housing Policy Council, a mortgage/housing-finance industry trade association, lobbying on housing-finance policy in Washington, DC. Urban Institute is DC-headquartered; the donor's listed location (St. Paul, MN 55101) is plausible as remote/home address but not independently confirmed — no distinct organization literally named 'Urban Institute' based in St. Paul was found (a separate St. Paul-based lobbyist also named 'Todd Hill,' of Hill Capitol Strategies, was checked and ruled out as a mismatch since his employer is not 'Urban Institute'). His high FEC donation count (155) and near-even partisan lean are consistent with a Washington housing-finance-industry government-affairs professional who gives to officials on both sides of housing/finance committees, but no FEC record could be independently verified (FEC.gov/OpenFEC API was rate-limited during research).

affiliations.snippet: "Todd served six years as senior director of government affairs with the Housing Policy Council, where he advocated on key mortgage and housing finance marketplace interests in legislative, regulatory, and judicial forums."

name: Hootnick, Adam
resolved_employer: Resonance Story Company (founder) — documentary filmmaker/director, Austin, TX 78756
evidence: Adam Hootnick is an Austin-based documentary filmmaker (Harvard College, Harvard Law School) who founded Resonance Story Company, a branded-documentary production house. His first feature, 'UNSETTLED,' followed young Israelis across the political/religious spectrum during the 2005 Gaza withdrawal and won the Slamdance Grand Jury Prize — this is journalistic/filmmaking work, not membership or leadership in an advocacy organization, so it is not recorded as a policy affiliation per the instructions. No board membership, PAC donation record, or TEC lobbyist registration found under this name.
